differentials.name: Discrete pelvic mass = endometrioma
differentials.raw: Discrete pelvic mass = endometrioma (in up to 10%)

differentials.name: Hormonal therapy (for pelvic pain / dyspareunia) to create a state of pseudopregnancy / pseudo-menopause / chronic anovulation: danocrine (Danazol®),
differentials.raw: Hormonal therapy (for pelvic pain / dyspareunia) to create a state of pseudopregnancy / pseudo-menopause / chronic anovulation: danocrine (Danazol®),

differentials.name: Hemorrhagic ovarian cyst
differentials.raw: Hemorrhagic ovarian cyst (acute symptoms, more complex cyst with clot retraction, thin fibrin stranding, resolution in 4-6 weeks)

differentials.name: Dermoid cyst
differentials.raw: Dermoid cyst (calcification, fat-fluid level, hyperechoic areas)

differentials.name: rectal pain / hematochezia
differentials.raw: rectal pain / hematochezia [ khezein, Greek = defecate]

differentials.name: urinary obstruction, flank pain Path:   initially serosal endometriotic deposits that may infiltrate into bladder / ureteral wall
differentials.raw: urinary obstruction, flank pain Path:   initially serosal endometriotic deposits that may infiltrate into bladder / ureteral wall

differentials.name: pleuritic chest pain, cyclic hemoptysis Cause:   microembolization
differentials.raw: pleuritic chest pain, cyclic hemoptysis Cause:   microembolization (via lymphatics or vascular channels), peritoneal-pleural migration (through diaphragmatic defects)

differentials.name: focal pain / tenderness associated with menses Location:   laparotomy scar, cervical biopsy / electrocautery, episiotomy scar, umbilicus
differentials.raw: focal pain / tenderness associated with menses Location:   laparotomy scar, cervical biopsy / electrocautery, episiotomy scar, umbilicus

context: Morphologic types: Dx:   laparoscopy / surgery ← limitations of imaging Rx: GnRH agonist (Lupron®), oral contraceptive pills implant recurrence in 28% by 18 months + in 40% by 9 years; adhesion recurrence in 40-50% Diffuse Endometriosis Path:  punctate small foci / stellate patches of < 2 cm initiating inflammatory response → organizing hemorrhage, fibrosis, adhesions US (low sensitivity): √  in 70% normal = often no detectable abnormality (when lesions small + scattered) MR (71% sensitive, 82% specific): √  frequently hyperintense multiple cysts bilaterally with thick walls on T1WI √  foci of
